buggy:
<M extends Map<String, String>> M putIn(final M map) {
    for (final Entry<String, Integer> entry : mapping.entrySet()) {
        final int col = entry.getValue().intValue();
        if (col < values.length) {
            map.put(entry.getKey(), values[col]);
        }
    }
    return map;
}
fix:
<M extends Map<String, String>> M putIn(final M map) {
    if (mapping == null || mapping.size() == 0){
        return map;
    }
    for (final Entry<String, Integer> entry : mapping.entrySet()) {
        final int col = entry.getValue().intValue();
        if (col < values.length) {
            map.put(entry.getKey(), values[col]);
        }
    }
    return map;
}
issue_title: CSVRecord.toMap() throws NPE on formats with no headers.
issue_description:
The method toMap() on CSVRecord throws a NullPointerExcpetion when called on records derived using a format with no headers.
The method documentation states a null map should be returned instead.
buggy_fl:
<M extends Map<String, String>> M putIn(final M map) {
    for (final Entry<String, Integer> entry : mapping.entrySet()) { /* bug is here */
        final int col = entry.getValue().intValue();
        if (col < values.length) {
            map.put(entry.getKey(), values[col]);
        }
    }
    return map;
}
